candidates.candidate: Decision questions and draft review must protect user choices before filing or execution.
candidates.decision_reason: Strong source support and clear transfer value for scope-changing workflows.

candidates.candidate: Define trigger boundaries before a workflow can claim ownership of a task.
candidates.decision_reason: Useful and supported, but less distinctive than the decision-brief mechanism.

candidates.candidate: Separate method body from runtime shell when reading engineering Skill materials.
candidates.decision_reason: Central to ReaderLab's cleaned-body requirement, but must preserve safety constraints in audit.

candidates.candidate: Adopt gstack's telemetry, routing injection, vendored migration, and artifact sync prompts as ReaderLab defaults.
candidates.decision_reason: Foreign runtime shell is not transferable under this demo's boundaries and would add state-changing behavior.

candidates.candidate: Create a reusable decision-brief Skill from the AskUserQuestion pattern.
candidates.decision_reason: Promising pattern, but this demo has not defined independent trigger/input/output/evidence tightly enough for a formal Skill.

candidates.candidate: Ask readers where they would draw the line between body method and execution shell.
candidates.decision_reason: Useful for reader discussion, but not a standalone method card.

candidates.candidate: Require verified code or project evidence before technical questions in a spec workflow.
candidates.decision_reason: Strong source support and directly improves executability of specs.
